meaning: n.[建]装饰用的灰泥vt.涂以灰泥,粉刷
examples: Their marbles and their stones are often actually painted stucco.
A porch stretched across the front of the tiny brown stucco house, four rooms, two down, two up.
A thick, square, iron pyrite mirror, inlaid with stucco plaques, was placed next to the female's right knee.
In its place, he created an L-shaped house with taupe stucco and dry-stacked-slate exteriors.
This stunning stucco home has 6 bedrooms and is located on 3 acres in Tucson.
